title: Consumer Goroutines, Cancellation, and Shutdown · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A Go pairing room incrementally implements a leak-resistant Go consumer lifecycle state machine, first with deterministic values and then with a changed failure, while keeping prior messaging invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a leak-resistant Go consumer lifecycle state machine while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Report ready only after connection, topology, channel, prefetch, consume registration, and required dependencies are ready; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Track consumer tags and channel generations so stale cancellation, delivery, and close events cannot mutate current state; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Treat a closed delivery channel and consumer-cancellation notifications as lifecycle events with cause, readiness, recovery, and ownership evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Consume Registration Readiness
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go messaging team handles case 5292 during table-driven topology review for a tenant migration. Build deterministic Go evidence to sequence consumer readiness, cancellation, handler draining, deadline, and close; reject stale connection and channel generations; then lower the byte and goroutine budget and preserve rejection evidence. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Consume Registration Readiness.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for report ready only after connection, topology, channel, prefetch, consume registration, and required dependencies are ready; in go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence. For RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Consume Registration Readiness, keep Consume Registration Readiness connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.
